articles.title: Maybe We Were Too Hard On Ronda
articles.description: Chael Sonnen has spent days criticizing different parts of Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano… but now he’s asking himself a different question: Was he unfair to Ronda? Chael compares the situation to Vitor Belfort vs Evander Holyfield — a fight where Vitor was heavily criticized afterward, despite going into the matchup believing he was facing a dangerous legend. According to Chael, Ronda may have been in the exact same position. From the outside, Gina looked like a legitimate opponent: bigger, accomplished, respected, and dangerous on paper. But once the weigh-in happened and Gina came in five pounds underweight, the entire reality of the matchup changed. Chael explains why Ronda’s aggressive strategy suddenly made perfect sense, why she immediately rushed the takedown, and why fans may have blamed the wrong person after the fight. There’s also a deeper conversation here about matchmaking, nostalgia fights, and the uncomfortable truth that many fans tune in hoping to watch someone fail. In Chael’s eyes, Ronda didn’t do anything wrong. She treated Gina like a threat — and handled her accordingly. #UFC #RondaRousey #GinaCarano #MMA #ChaelSonnen 0:00 “Have I been too hard on Ronda?”0:18 Why Chael felt justified criticizing the fight0:52 The Vitor vs Holyfield comparison1:40 Why Vitor wasn’t wrong2:18 Ronda respected Gina3:00 The weigh-in changed everything3:42 Gina exposed herself at the scale4:10 Why Ronda rushed the fight4:40 Ronda was scared to trade5:08 “You don’t know until they quit”5:40 Gina wants another fight?6:08 The dangerous matchmaking mindset6:52 Fans weren’t tuning in to cheer7:20 Why promoters sell “magic beans”7:48 “Ronda did nothing wrong” Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Why McGregor vs Holloway Doesn’t Add Up #ufc
articles.description: Conor McGregor has headlined some of the biggest fights in MMA history. But according to this breakdown, there’s one major issue with a potential fight against Max Holloway — nobody can explain why the fight truly matters. Big UFC main events usually need one of two things: a championship belt or genuine bad blood. This matchup has neither. McGregor already owns a win over Holloway, and there doesn’t appear to be any personal grudge between them. That creates a difficult challenge, even for someone as legendary at promotion as Conor. Holloway is one of the most respected fighters in the sport, and fans generally love both guys. So where does the tension come from? That’s the fascinating part of this conversation. The pressure falls entirely on McGregor to create intrigue, emotion, and urgency around the fight. If anyone can sell a matchup that seems impossible to market, it’s Conor McGregor. But even then, this could be one of the toughest promotional puzzles of his career. Can Conor convince fans this is more than just nostalgia? Or does this fight need something bigger attached to it before fans fully buy in? Subscribe for more UFC analysis, fight breakdowns, and Chael-style MMA commentary. #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #UFC #MMA #FightTalk #CombatSports #ChaelSonnen

articles.title: Conor’s Puncher Chance Isn’t Enough #ufc
articles.description: Conor McGregor vs. Max Holloway is the kind of fight that instantly grabs attention—but the deeper you analyze it, the harder it becomes to imagine how McGregor wins. In this clip, the discussion centers around the famous “puncher’s chance” argument. Fans say it every week in combat sports: one punch can change everything. And if anyone deserves belief in that possibility, it’s Conor McGregor. But there’s a major problem. The opponent is Max Holloway. For years, Holloway built a reputation as one of the most durable fighters in MMA history. Before his loss to Ilia Topuria, Holloway had never even been knocked down, let alone knocked out. Meanwhile, questions continue to surround McGregor’s cardio, inactivity, and recovery after a devastating leg injury. That’s what makes this matchup so fascinating. Nobody realistically expects McGregor to win a five-round decision after years away from the sport. The path to victory would almost certainly rely on his legendary left hand and the ability to create one perfect moment. And against Max Holloway, even that may not be enough. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #UFC #MMA #IliaTopuria #FightTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: Jake Paul Earned More Respect Than Francis...
articles.description: Francis Ngannou and Jake Paul had a strange interaction over the weekend and Chael Sonnen thinks it revealed a much bigger problem. What exactly does Francis want right now? Chael breaks down the confusing direction Francis took after his latest fight, bouncing from Jon Jones and MMA… directly into Jake Paul and boxing. According to Chael, those are two completely different businesses, two completely different audiences, and ultimately two completely different futures. There’s also discussion about why Jon Jones wasn’t brought into the cage for a faceoff, why Jake Paul’s response to Francis completely shut down the exchange, and why Chael believes Francis is now in a position where he has to clearly tell fans which sport he actually wants to represent. Chael also questions whether Jake should even be fighting massive heavyweights anymore after surviving Anthony Joshua and leaving with a double broken jaw. The bigger issue isn’t whether Francis vs Jake is interesting. It’s what that fight would cost MMA if it actually happens. #UFC #MMA #FrancisNgannou #JakePaul #JonJones #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Francis and Jake’s strange interaction 0:42 The Jon Jones confusion 1:14 Why no faceoff happened 1:50 MMA vs boxing 2:20 Francis is unique in combat sports 3:10 Francis tries teasing Jake 3:40 Jake’s brutal response 4:26 “Would you actually rather see this?” 5:08 The Anthony Joshua comparison 5:34 Jake’s double broken jaw 6:02 “Jake already proved his courage” 6:40 Why Francis calling out Jake feels strange 7:12 Francis has to choose a side 7:48 Why fans need clarity now Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Are Netflix’s MMA Numbers Actually Good?
articles.description: Netflix reportedly pulled around 12 million viewers for Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano — but Chael Sonnen thinks everyone is avoiding the real question: Was that actually good? Chael breaks down the strange way people are talking about the numbers, why phrases like “good for MMA” may actually reveal the problem, and how Jake Paul vs Mike Tyson completely changed the conversation around combat sports on Netflix. If boxing reportedly did TEN TIMES the numb ers, what does that mean for MMA moving forward? Chael also dives into the business side of the sport, whether Netflix and MVP can realistically build repeatable MMA events, and why keeping a fight card together may have secretly been the biggest accomplishment of the entire weekend. There’s also discussion about Jake Paul potentially fighting Francis Ngannou, Mike Perry calling for an MMA fight, and why combat sports are becoming more about personalities and moments than the sport itself. The bigger question isn’t whether Ronda vs Gina succeeded. It’s whether Netflix believes MMA is worth investing in again. #UFC #MMA #Netflix #RondaRousey #JakePaul #FrancisNgannou #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Netflix did 12 million views 0:35 “Is that actually good?” 1:00 Jake Paul vs Tyson comparison 1:42 Boxing vs MMA on Netflix 2:00 Jake Paul accepts BOTH fights 2:42 Why Tyson can’t be duplicated 3:26 Is MMA repeatable on Netflix? 4:18 Was Gina even necessary? 5:00 “You’d do it again for $5 million?” 5:34 Could women’s MMA still work here? 6:08 Is Ronda really done? 6:40 Francis vs Jon Jones CAN happen 7:00 MVP’s most underrated accomplishment 7:48 Why UFC fighters miss weight so often 8:32 The UFC’s unique problem 9:18 “Defend the 12 million number” Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.description: On a SPECIAL EDITION of the DC Check-In, Daniel Cormier sits down with UFC superstar Mauricio Ruffy ahead of his fight against Michael Chandler at the White House for the UFC's Freedom 250 event. Ruffy breaks down his matchup against Chandler and explains why a knockout result will put him next in line for a title shot against Ilia Topuria over Arman Tsarukyan. articles.title: Colby Covington, Cut or Retired?
articles.description: Reports started circulating that Colby Covington had been cut from the UFC but according to Chael Sonnen, that’s not what happened at all. Chael explains the massive difference between being released by the UFC and officially notifying the organization of retirement. On the surface, they may sound similar. In reality, they create completely different futures for a fighter. If Colby had been released, he’d immediately become one of the hottest free agents in combat sports. But by retiring, his UFC contract effectively freezes meaning he can’t simply go fight somewhere else whenever he wants. Chael also breaks down the testing pool, whereabouts violations, mandatory re-entry timelines, and why formally leaving the system is a much bigger step than most fans realize. There’s also a deeper layer here involving Real American Freestyle, Colby’s recent comments about stepping away from MMA, and why this move may have been more serious than people initially believed. #UFC #ColbyCovington #MMA #ChaelSonnen #RealAmericanFreestyle 0:00 Two completely different stories 0:42 Colby’s RAF announcement 1:20 Why leaving the testing pool matters 2:04 The consequences of retirement 2:28 Jon Jones and reentering the pool 2:50 “Give my spot to somebody else” 3:06 What happens if Colby was released 3:40 Colby’s value on the open market 4:08 The definition of “retirement” 4:44 Why the contract freezes 5:14 What happens if Colby returns 5:40 The inaccurate reporting 6:05 The HUGE distinction explained Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Ronda’s Advice Makes NO Sense
articles.description: Ronda Rousey recently told young fighters not to go to the UFC until they’re truly ready — but Chael Sonnen thinks that advice completely falls apart the second you examine it closely. Chael breaks down why success can sometimes make people forget how difficult the climb really was, and why Ronda’s perspective may unintentionally ignore the brutal reality most fighters face on the regional scene. If a young fighter finally gets the call from the UFC… what exactly are they supposed to do instead? Turn it down and fight the same killers for less money, less exposure, and no opportunity? Chael explains why the competition outside the UFC is often just as dangerous, using stories from his own career fighting names like Trevor Prangley and Babalu long before the bright lights ever showed up. There’s also a deeper point underneath all of this: fighters are never truly “ready.” Sometimes the opportunity comes first — and you figure the rest out afterward. #UFC #MMA #RondaRousey #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Why successful people give strange advice 0:36 Ronda tells fighters to avoid the UFC 1:12 What her advice implies 1:55 “Then what am I supposed to do?” 2:42 The regional scene is just as hard 3:20 Why fighters get treated as second-class 4:02 Chael’s direct question to Ronda 4:44 Fighting the same guys anyway 5:08 The fake tough guy business 5:49 What Chael DOES agree with6:08 The flaw in Ronda’s logic 6:27 “Nobody’s ever really ready” Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Ronda’s Victory Lap Felt 'Cringey'
articles.description: Matt Brown said Ronda Rousey didn’t redeem herself… and Chael thinks he understands exactly why fans reacted so negatively. This wasn’t really about the fight itself. It was about the emotional payoff people expected — and never got. Chael breaks down the strange psychology behind Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano, why audiences subconsciously needed to see devastation from one side, and how both women reacted in the exact opposite way fans wanted. Gina looked relieved and content in defeat. Ronda celebrated the win like everything in MMA had finally been healed. That disconnect is what made the entire event feel uncomfortable for so many viewers. Chael also asks a deeper question: what actually matters more in a fighter’s legacy? Winning a nostalgia fight on Netflix… or losing in a world title fight against one of the greatest champions ever? This isn’t just about Ronda. It’s about how fans process endings, redemption arcs, and what combat sports audiences really want — whether they admit it or not. #UFC #RondaRousey #GinaCarano #MMA #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Matt Brown’s “cringy” comment0:38 What fans actually wanted1:31 Gina reacted the wrong way2:05 Ronda reacted the wrong way2:42 Why fans felt uncomfortable3:18 “Everything is okay in MMA”4:05 Which career moment is better?5:26 Did Ronda really accomplish something? articles.title: Why Conor vs Holloway Is So Hard to Sell
articles.description: Conor McGregor vs Max Holloway sounds massive on paper. So why does Chael Sonnen think it’s such a difficult fight to explain? Chael breaks down the unusual reality surrounding a possible Conor vs Holloway matchup — a fight without a title, without a grudge, and without the normal ingredients that make blockbuster MMA main events work. According to Chael, big fights almost always need one of two things: a championship… or real personal conflict. This fight has neither. Conor already beat Max years ago. The two don’t dislike each other. Max hasn’t spent years chasing revenge. And now the UFC could be putting them together at a weight class neither guy truly belongs in. So what exactly is the story? Chael dives into why Conor has historically been the perfect promoter’s dream, why McGregor’s trash talk worked differently against Jose Aldo and Floyd Mayweather, and why this may be one of the hardest fights Conor has ever had to “sell.” He also proposes a possible solution involving Michael Chandler — and explains why every great fight ultimately has to lead somewhere meaningful. Because if this fight happens… the UFC still has to answer one question: Why are we here? #UFC #MMA #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #MichaelChandler #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Conor vs Max confusion0:38 How big fights are actually built1:46 Why main events need belts or grudges3:02 The “Money Belt” story4:30 Chael found the Mayweather belt5:52 Why Conor has a difficult job here6:42 Conor already beat Max7:38 Why Conor’s old trash talk worked9:08 Jose Aldo and Mayweather examples10:14 Why Max won’t sell the fight11:18 The weight class problem12:20 What happens if Max wins?13:14 Chael’s Michael Chandler angle14:32 “Why are we here?”15:28 Waiting for Conor’s answer Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Will We See Another Netflix MVP Event?
articles.description: Chael Sonnen thinks Ronda Rousey pulled off one of the smartest promotional performances of her career. Because Ronda vs Gina Carano was never really about Gina Carano. Chael breaks down why Ronda found a completely different angle to sell a fight that had no title, no rankings implications, no personal hatred, and no obvious future attached to it. Instead of pretending she had a grudge with Gina, Ronda turned the story into something bigger — Hunter Campbell, the UFC, MVP, and the idea of competing promotions. Even if none of it was actually real. According to Chael, that’s the entire point of fight promotion. He explains why fans often feel “fooled” when fighters later admit the rivalry wasn’t personal, why personal grudges remain the most powerful force in combat sports, and how fighters can still build compelling stories even when they don’t genuinely dislike each other. Chael also compares the situation to Sean Strickland vs Khamzat Chimaev, explaining why those fighters could still create massive interest without relying on fake hatred. Because in the fight business, the audience doesn’t just buy the matchup. They buy the angle. #UFC #MMA #RondaRousey #GinaCarano #ChaelSonnen #JakePaul 0:00 Ronda’s comment stuck with Chael0:36 “Favorite version” of Ronda1:18 Why the fight was hard to promote2:04 Ronda became the heel2:52 Ronda made it about the UFC3:48 Jake Paul reveals the truth4:42 Why fans feel “fooled”5:34 Ronda’s angle explained6:22 How do you sell fights without hate?7:12 Pereira and Holgate example8:04 Why personal grudges matter most9:06 Sean vs Chimaev comparison10:10 You can sell fights different ways11:02 “They buy the angle” Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Conor’s Comeback Has Never Been Done #ufc
articles.description: Conor McGregor vs. Max Holloway at International Fight Week would be more than just a blockbuster matchup—it would be one of the greatest comeback attempts in MMA history. In this clip, the focus is on the impossible mountain McGregor would have to climb to win. Fighters have returned from devastating leg injuries before, including Anderson Silva and Chris Weidman. They remained dangerous competitors, but none ever fully returned to the level they once reached before the injury. That’s obstacle number one. Obstacle number two is the layoff. No fighter has successfully sat out for six years and returned against a current elite contender at the highest level. And then comes obstacle number three: the weight class. McGregor would reportedly be competing outside his natural division against an active, dangerous opponent still fighting at the top of the sport. That’s what makes this fight so compelling. The challenge isn’t just physical—it’s historical. McGregor wouldn’t simply be trying to win a fight. He’d be attempting something MMA history says shouldn’t be possible. And that’s exactly why fans can’t stop talking about it. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #UFC #MMA #InternationalFightWeek #FightTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: Jon Jones and Francis Could Fight Tonight #ufc
articles.description: Francis Ngannou vs. Jon Jones remains one of the biggest “what if” fights in combat sports—and in this clip, the argument is that nothing is truly stopping it from happening. The discussion dives into why the matchup still captures attention years later while also criticizing Ngannou’s approach to building interest around his fights. The point being made is simple: when fans only care about one possible opponent, that creates a major promotional problem. And that opponent is Jon Jones. The clip also touches on how differently Jones presents himself publicly compared to the larger-than-life aura surrounding the matchup. According to the argument here, Jones and Ngannou could theoretically fight at any time if both sides genuinely wanted it badly enough, regardless of contracts or promotional politics. That’s what makes the situation so fascinating. Because outside of Jon Jones, there may not be another opponent that creates the same excitement for Ngannou. And with heavyweight storylines constantly shifting between MMA and boxing, the pressure to finally make this fight only keeps growing. Fans have wanted this matchup for years—and they still haven’t let it go. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #JonJones #FrancisNgannou #UFC #MMA #Heavyweight #CombatSports #FightTalk

articles.title: Francis and Jon Don’t Want to Fight
articles.description: Francis Ngannou vs Jon Jones keeps getting talked about. Chael Sonnen thinks that’s exactly the point. Chael breaks down the strange segment involving Francis, Jon Jones, Ariel Helwani, and Jake Paul — and explains why the interview may have been more important than the fight itself. According to Chael, Jon Jones already did everything required to make the Francis fight happen years ago. He vacated the light heavyweight title, spent years bulking up for heavyweight, and fully expected Francis to be waiting for him when he arrived. Instead, Francis left. Now the narrative has somehow shifted into contracts, promotions, and legal complications — but Chael argues none of that is actually stopping the fight from happening if both men truly wanted it. He also explains why the entire segment exposed the difference between being a real promoter and simply being an event planner, why Jake Paul’s presence changed the energy instantly, and why Francis still struggles to understand how to build meaningful fights outside the cage. Because right now, Ngannou faces a difficult reality: The biggest fight available is against a man everyone pretends he can’t fight. And the other option might be his own boss. #UFC #MMA #JonJones #FrancisNgannou #JakePaul #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Francis vs Lins reaction 0:42 Why the segment mattered more than the fight 1:30 Lins actually did his job 2:14 Promoter vs event planner 3:18 Ariel brings Jon Jones into it 4:10 Jon takes the bait 5:02 Jon originally came to heavyweight for Francis 6:08 Jon’s hands are clean 7:00 Francis left when the fight was ready 8:04 “They can fight right now” 9:02 They don’t actually want to fight10:08 Winning public perception matters more11:18 Why Jake Paul changed the segment12:12 Francis doesn’t know how to build fights13:06 Nobody wants to see Francis vs Lins14:02 The real cliffhanger moving forward Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Most Insulting Part of Ronda vs Gina
articles.description: Gina Carano got up smiling. And for Chael Sonnen, that may have been the most revealing moment of the entire night. Chael breaks down the strange emotional dynamic surrounding Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano — not just the fight itself, but the energy, the body language, and the feeling that Gina was genuinely enjoying every second of it. Because there’s something uniquely insulting about watching somebody smile in the middle of chaos. Chael explains why Ronda was put in one of the hardest promotional positions imaginable: no belt, no title, no rankings stakes, no real personal grudge, and a crowd heavily leaning toward Gina from the beginning. Yet somehow, the fight still carried tension. He also dives into the larger Netflix and MVP presentation, why the show exceeded expectations from a production standpoint, and whether this experiment could turn into something much bigger moving forward. But the moment that stuck with Chael wasn’t the production. It wasn’t the commentary team. It wasn’t even the result. It was Gina Carano standing there… smiling. #UFC #MMA #GinaCarano #RondaRousey #ChaelSonnen #JakePaul 0:00 Gina Carano smiling stood out 0:32 Why the fight wasn’t the real story 1:12 Netflix and MVP worked surprisingly well 2:08 Was the event actually successful? 3:20 Why subscriber retention matters 4:08 Chael praises the presentation 5:02 Kenny Florian and Mauro reaction 5:42 Jon Jones, Cain, and Tyron appearances 6:38 Why Gina winning mattered 7:18 Holly Holm and Cyborg possibilities 8:10 Ronda chasing “the fix” 9:08 Will Ronda fight again? 10:00 The myth of Ronda vs Gina 11:02 Why this matchup never truly existed 12:04 Everybody left whole 12:42 “Will there be another event?” 13:18 Gina’s smile changed the feeling of the night Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Dana White Thought Strickland Won #ufc
articles.description: Sean Strickland vs. Khamzat Chimaev may be one of the closest UFC title fights in recent memory—and even now, people still don’t fully agree on who won. In this clip, the focus shifts to the reaction after the fight and why the rematch debate has become so complicated. There are fans who believe Chimaev deserved the decision, but there hasn’t been the overwhelming outrage that normally follows a controversial scorecard. Even UFC CEO Dana White publicly said he scored the fight for Strickland. And inside the arena, the crowd reaction told a similar story. That’s why this creates such an interesting situation for the middleweight division. Strickland’s options now appear to come down to two names: Chimaev or Nassourdine Imavov. One fight is dangerous, emotional, and demands a rematch. The other may offer a cleaner stylistic matchup and a clearer path forward. Because at championship level, the goal isn’t just proving toughness. The goal is maximizing opportunity—biggest paycheck, best matchup, smartest move. And now Strickland has to decide which path he wants. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #DanaWhite #UFC #MMA #Middleweight #FightTalk

articles.title: Daniel Cormier & Ben Askren REACT to Ronda Rousey SUBMITTING Gina Carano, WILD Francis KO, Nate Diaz
articles.description: Ronda Rousey submitted Gina Carano in just 17 SECONDS to win by an armbar submission in Netflix's MVP MMA card's main event. Friend of the show Ben Askren joins Daniel Cormier in breaking down the Ronda-Gina fight, and giving their reaction to the rest of the card! The guys are adamant that Francis Ngannou needs a more challenging opponent after knocking out Philipe Lins in the first round, despite being 39 years old. Also, the guys talk about Mike Perry's brutal TKO win vs. Nate Diaz and Nate's vintage post-fight rant after the loss. And don't miss the guys break down the card and compare the Netflix and Jake Paul MVP product to the UFC's. All lines provided by @hardrockbet Follow me on all social media platforms!
